name: BCG vaccine against tuberculosis (Colditz 1994)
citation: Colditz GA, Brewer TF, Berkey CS, et al. Efficacy of BCG vaccine in the prevention of tuberculosis. JAMA 1994;271(9):698-702.
data_provenance: Raw 2x2 counts as distributed in metafor's dat.bcg (Viechtbauer W. Conducting meta-analyses in R with the metafor package. J Stat Softw 2010;36(3):1-48). ai=vaccinated TB+, n1=vaccinated total, ci=control TB+, n2=control total.

reference.source: metafor rma(measure='RR', method='REML', data=dat.bcg) — standard published output (Viechtbauer 2010 and metafor documentation).

note: Reproduction target is the random-effects pooled logRR. PM (Paule-Mandel) is used by the engine (advanced-stats rule: not DL for small k); the published reference uses REML, whose point estimate agrees to <0.001 on this dataset. tau^2 is method-dependent (DL 0.309 / PM 0.318 / REML 0.313), so its tolerance is wider. Re-verify the raw counts against a fresh metafor dat.bcg before citing externally.
